page: Amazon Web Services x1e.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 8 vCPUs and 244.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 30.5 GB per core. Powered by an Intel Xeon E7-8880 v3 processor running at 2.3 GHz, this instance utilizes the Xen hypervisor and provides a dedicated CPU allocation across 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor includes a 45 MB L3 cache and supports AVX, AES, and FMA instruction sets, though it lacks newer AVX-512 extensions.

For local storage, the instance is equipped with a 240 GB NVMe SSD. Networking capabilities are limited, with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included, and there are no hardware GPU accelerators.

Benchmark data indicates a clear tradeoff between massive memory capacity and processing speed. The instance scores in the bottom 10% for single-core CPU performance and the bottom 25% for multi-core operations across stress-ng and Geekbench tests. Memory bandwidth also falls into the bottom 25%, with read-only large block bandwidth measured at 36.34 GB/sec. Consequently, tasks requiring high-throughput computational power or rapid memory access speeds will face performance bottlenecks.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the x1e.2xlarge is positioned as a specialized resource density play, offering a massive memory footprint relative to its virtual core count. This makes it a qualitative fit for memory-bound workloads that do not require high CPU performance, such as hosting large in-memory databases, enterprise caching layers, or legacy applications with high RAM requirements but low parallel processing demands.
description: Amazon Web Services x1e.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance designed for workloads requiring a massive RAM footprint relative to compute resources. It features 8 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon E7-8880 v3 processor at 2.3 GHz, paired with 244.0 GB of RAM and a 240 GB NVMe SSD. Operating on the Xen hypervisor, the instance provides dedicated CPU allocation but exhibits clear performance tradeoffs. Benchmark results place its single-core and multi-core CPU performance in the lower percentiles, and memory bandwidth remains relatively low. With a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps and no GPU acceleration, this instance is not suited for high-performance computing. Instead, its resource profile is qualitatively cost-effective for memory-bound, low-throughput applications, such as in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise software requiring high memory capacity without intensive CPU utilization.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x1e.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 8 vCPUs, 244 GB RAM, and a 240 GB NVMe SSD, designed for memory-intensive workloads with low compute demands.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services x1e.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 244 GB RAM, and a 240 GB NVMe SSD for memory-bound workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server delivering an exceptionally high RAM-to-vCPU ratio and local NVMe storage for memory-bound enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 8 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon E7-8880 v3 processor at 2.3 GHz
Extremely high memory capacity of 244.0 GB RAM
Includes 240 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps on the Xen hypervisor
Best suited for in-memory databases and memory-intensive caching layers
